name: Current Affairs in Geography of India (7): The Delhi-Dehradun Expressway & India's Expressway Network

sourceRefs.label: blackridgeresearch.com/studyiq.com - Delhi-Dehradun Expressway inauguration (14 April 2026); India's expressway network status (April 2026); Delhi-Mumbai Expressway

researchNotes: Seventh Geography current-affairs topic (7th lap). Picked a dated infrastructure INAUGURATION (stable, fixed fact) over the monsoon/rainfall forecast data found in initial research — forecasts are exactly the kind of volatile, soon-to-be-outdated statistic flagged as lower-priority since Day 58.

reading.intro: Covers the newly inaugurated Delhi-Dehradun Expressway and the broader status of India's expressway network.

qa.question: When was the Delhi-Dehradun Expressway inaugurated, and by whom?
qa.answer: 14 April 2026, by Prime Minister Narendra Modi.

qa.question: How long is the Delhi-Dehradun Expressway, and how much does it reduce travel time to?
qa.answer: 210 km, reducing Delhi-Dehradun travel time to just 2.5 hours.

qa.question: What are some key engineering features of the Delhi-Dehradun Expressway?
qa.answer: 6 entry/exit points, 113 underpasses, 76 km of service roads, 29 km of elevated highways, 5 Road-Over-Bridges (ROBs), and 62 bus shelters.

qa.question: As of April 2026, what is the total length of India's completed expressway network, and how much more is under construction?
What is the Delhi-Mumbai Expressway, and what will it achieve once fully complete?
qa.answer: India's most ambitious road project — 1,350 km, costing ₹1,00,000 crore — which will cut Delhi-Mumbai travel time from ~24 hours to just 12 hours once fully complete (partially opened in phases since 2023).
